title: Curate the 10-15 representative spike tasks
description:
# Curate the 10–15 representative spike tasks

## What

Produce the actual task set the spike will run. Each task should be a real Plexus workflow that an agent might be asked to perform.

## Coverage requirements

The set must collectively exercise:

- Read-only listing / info / details (~2 tasks)
- Single-item prediction (~1 task)
- Multi-step composition (e.g., find recent feedback → analyze → produce summary, ~2 tasks)
- Long-running operation invocation with streamed progress (~2 tasks, against the stubbed evaluation.run)
- Budget-aware decisions (e.g., a task where the budget is tight enough that the agent should pick the cheaper tool, ~2 tasks)
- Error handling (e.g., a task involving an item that doesn't exist, ~1 task)
- HITL-by-default trigger (a destructive op the agent must approve — even though we're stubbing, the flow should be exercised, ~1 task)
- Discovery flow (a task whose solution requires the agent to call plexus.docs.get for a non-obvious namespace, ~2 tasks)

## Per-task artifact

Each task is a YAML or JSON file with:
- id
- name
- prompt (what the user asks the agent)
- expected_outcome (what success looks like, machine-checkable where possible)
- coverage_tags (which categories above this exercises)
- max_attempts_for_first_try_pass (default 1)

## Deliverable

A `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/tasks/` directory with the task definitions, ready to be consumed by the test harness.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text:
Progress: curated the representative spike task set under `spikes/runtime-mcp-validation/tasks/`.

Created 15 YAML task definitions plus a README/coverage matrix. The set covers read-only scorecard/score inspection, score prediction, feedback pattern summarization, evaluation comparison, streaming evaluation run, async evaluation handle behavior, budget-aware triage, cheap-score-before-LLM selection, structured error handling, HITL-required champion promotion, docs-driven dataset and report workflows, dataset association checks, and procedure/chat introspection.

Validation run: parsed all YAML definitions with PyYAML and checked required fields, unique IDs, non-empty coverage tags and required APIs, and first-try max attempts = 1. Result: `validated_files=15`, `unique_ids=15`.

Task remains `in_progress` until the changes are reviewed and landed.
